flow_divergence: codex CLI syntax is `codex exec` not `codex -q` as documented in FLOW.md — had to discover this mid-review
friction: post-build review caught real issues but required a second build pass to fix blockers before verify
suggestion: add a bash helper for JSONL appends so agents don't hand-craft JSON

friction: Codex background task output was hard to parse — had to grep for the review verdict
suggestion: consider a structured output format for codex exec reviews

friction: security hook required innerHTML refactor to DOM methods — good guard but added a build pass
suggestion: consider adding the viewer to the README root layout section

friction: awk multiline vars and grep regex interpretation caused two debugging rounds
suggestion: add a viewer rebuild to the SOD refresh script so both stay in sync

flow_divergence: committed a mid-build edit (drop zone removal) without updating the spec first — had to fold it back in
friction: bash markdown parsing is tedious but necessary for zero-dependency; Codex caught four real issues in the viewer rendering
suggestion: consider a workflow-discipline reminder when the agent edits files not mentioned in the current spec's acceptance criteria

friction: first test used an exempt file which silently passed — had to rethink what constitutes a real scope violation test
suggestion: exempt patterns should be documented more prominently so test authors know which files to avoid

flow_divergence: skipped Codex peer review — CLI stalled after reading files, proceeded without
friction: none — review-only spec, no code to wrestle with
suggestion: the viewer pipeline is the top condensation target

friction: SOD script ordering matters — viewer rebuild can invalidate SOD after staging
suggestion: FLOW.md needs trimming to hit 3k bootstrap target

flow_divergence: human-steered: added version bump and noted human guidance convention mid-build rather than creating new specs
friction: first FLOW.md rewrite dropped 4 rules — Codex caught all of them
suggestion: add a diff-based rule checker that verifies key phrases survive rewrites

friction: peer-review CLI calls completed but were slow enough to look stalled, which makes the required review step feel brittle during review-only work
suggestion: document a timeout-and-proceed rule for peer reviews when the other CLI hangs or returns too slowly

friction: parallel Claude spec reviews collided on temp-file handling, so the backlog review step had to fall back to sequential peer reviews
suggestion: document that cross-model reviews should run sequentially when the other CLI uses shared temp files

friction: the fix was trivial once the source of truth was pinned to FLOW.md
suggestion: keep agent-specific files as thin adapters to FLOW.md so command drift has fewer places to survive

friction: verifying embedded viewer data took a little extra work because the generated HTML stores everything on one large line
suggestion: add a small repo-local check that parses EMBEDDED_CHANGES and asserts known archive timestamps so viewer regressions are easier to catch

friction: the first test pass exposed that closeout discipline depends on staged/index state as much as file contents, so the fixtures needed a second pass
suggestion: add more fixture helpers that model staged tracked-file additions before SOD refresh so closeout tests are less fragile

friction: the only wasted motion was a first reproduction run that used zsh's reserved status variable name
suggestion: keep using disposable repo fixtures for workflow validation so mistakes are reproducible without polluting the main checkout

friction: first implementation was too broad — bypassed all gates instead of just the spec gate
suggestion: gate bypass functions should document exactly which gates they exempt

friction: first test tested the wrong layer — called --check instead of the hook function
suggestion: none

friction: python-research self-containment vs line budget was the main tension
suggestion: none

friction: Generated viewer and sod artifacts both needed refresh while the active change file was still moving during verify.
suggestion: Keep generated artifact ownership centralized in the archive path and explicit freshness checks in validation.

friction: sod needed a second refresh because the new docs file had to be staged before it counted toward the generated metrics.
suggestion: For docs-only additions, stage new tracked files before rerunning the sod generator.

friction: The main risk was over-designing a new advisory taxonomy when the real gap was simply missing external-target verification.
suggestion: When a user says they want something live, make the target and verification method explicit in the spec.

friction: The main risk was over-specifying identity policy instead of just making publish authority explicit in specs and final reporting.
suggestion: For external-target work, record the expected account before attempting push or deploy.

friction: The fix itself was trivial; the real work was honoring the repo's full spec-review-verify-closeout loop for a release-facing wording cleanup.
suggestion: Include setup.sh in release-surface wording sweeps so onboarding text stays aligned with README and hooks.

friction: codex review caught upgrade-safety gap
suggestion: add migration checklist to rename specs

friction: prepare-commit-msg hook complicates test setup for config changes
BSD vs GNU sed portability gotcha in tests
suggestion: prefer awk+tmp-file or perl for cross-platform in-place edits in tests
consider making build-dust.sh a full template-based regenerator so structural changes don't need double-application

friction: grep -c exit-1-on-zero-matches interacting with set -e

flow_divergence: initially misread 'mochi only' as single-identity; corrected mid-flow via filter-repo
suggestion: pre-release review specs should explicitly name the target identity upfront

flow_divergence: v0.2.0 shipped without sod; gates don't cover git push or gh API calls
friction: cultural rules drift under momentum; no mechanical catch for external API writes
suggestion: if pre-push warning is ignored in practice, upgrade to block-by-default with SOD_PUBLISH_ACK=1 bypass

friction: set -e + command substitution; shim brace-expansion trap
suggestion: agents should prefer 'cmd && rc=0 || rc=$?' over 'x=$(cmd); rc=$?' under set -e
projects using wc -m on multibyte files should bake in a UTF-8 locale probe from day one

friction: locale spelling variance; set -o pipefail + head SIGPIPE trap
suggestion: when truncating large output in shell, write to temp file first; never pipe to head under pipefail
